## Page 260

### [p260_c0001] Limited
### [p260_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p260_c0003] B62
### [p260_c0004] Downtown Brooklyn - Astoria
[p260_c0005] Related Routes: B62, B27, B43, B57, Q63, Q66
### [p260_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p260_c0007] Existing: 7.6 miles Proposed: 8.7 miles
### [p260_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p260_c0009] Existing: 870 feet Proposed: 1,459 feet
### [p260_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p260_c0011] Existing: 2.5 per mile Proposed: 1.6 per mile
### [p260_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p260_c0013] Bus:
[p260_c0014] B25, B26, B27, B38, B39, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q18, Q19, Q39, Q54, Q59, Q63, Q66, Q67, Q68, Q69, Q104
### [p260_c0015] Subway:
[p260_c0016] 2 3 4 5 7 A C E M F G L N R
### [p260_c0017] LIRR:
[p260_c0018] Hunterspoint Avenue
### [p260_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p260_c0020] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed B62 would be extended north from Long Island City to Astoria via 21 St, providing new all-day frequent service between Brooklyn and Queens, responding to customer requests for better interborough service. Although the proposed B62 would no longer directly serve Queens Plaza and Queensborough Plaza, service would still be provided by the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q63 or Q66. In Brooklyn, the proposed B62 would use Flushing Av immediately adjacent to the Brooklyn Navy Yard, providing better connections from that employment center to the north. Service on Park Av would be provided by the proposed B57, and service to York St in Vinegar Hill would be provided by the proposed B27 and B67. Through Williamsburg, the proposed B62 would travel northbound on Bedford Av and southbound on Driggs Av and Lee Av, removing turns and reducing delays. Existing B62 service on Broadway, Division Av, and Kent Av/Wythe Av would be discontinued. The proposed B62 would travel on McGuinness Blvd between Nassau Av/Driggs Av and Freeman St/Green St to provide faster interborough travel. The B43 would continue to serve Manhattan Av.
[p260_c0021] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p260_c0022] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays to 10-minutes-or-better and service would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends. Overnight, the proposed B62 would serve York St in Vinegar Hill via Navy St and Gold St.
### [p260_c0023] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p260_c0024] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p260_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p260_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p260_c0027]

```text

```

## Page 269

### [p269_c0001] Local
### [p269_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p269_c0003] B64
### [p269_c0004] Sunset Park - Coney Island
[p269_c0005] Related Routes: B64, B4, B9, B82
### [p269_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p269_c0007] Existing: 6.7 miles Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p269_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p269_c0009] Existing: 726 feet Proposed: 1,032 feet
### [p269_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p269_c0011] Existing: 2.1 per mile Proposed: 2.4 per mile
### [p269_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p269_c0013] Bus:
[p269_c0014] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B9, B11, B16, B36, B37, B49, B63, B70, B74, B82, B82 SBS
### [p269_c0015] Subway:
[p269_c0016] D N R F Q
### [p269_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p269_c0018] The proposed B64 would be rerouted on its northern end to serve the Sunset Park Waterfront instead of Shore Rd. The route would travel via 4 Av and 60 St and provide new connections to the Brooklyn Army Terminal and NYU Langone Hospital Brooklyn from southern Brooklyn and from the future accessible 59 St N/R express subway station. The B9 would continue to serve Bay Ridge Av west of 4 Av. The proposed B64 would be rerouted on its southern end to travel via Cropsey Av instead of via Harway Av and Stillwell Av, replacing the B82 which is proposed to be shortened and removing a turn and reducing delays. Service on Stillwell Av would be provided by the proposed B4. Service on Harway Av would be discontinued.
[p269_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p269_c0020] Frequencies would be increased during some time periods on weekdays, Saturdays, and Sundays. Service span would be slightly increased.
### [p269_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p269_c0022] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access
### [p269_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p269_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

### [p273_c0003] B65
### [p273_c0004] Bergen/Dean Streets
[p273_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Crown Heights
[p273_c0006] Related Routes: B65, B15, B45
### [p273_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p273_c0008] Existing: 4.4 miles Proposed: 4.1 miles
### [p273_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p273_c0010] Existing: 742 feet Proposed: 1,013 feet

[p273_c0012] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p273_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p273_c0014] Bus:
[p273_c0015] B15, B25, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B45, B46, B47, B48, B49, B52, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B67, B69
### [p273_c0016] Subway:
[p273_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G
### [p273_c0018] LIRR:
[p273_c0019] Atlantic Terminal
### [p273_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p273_c0021] The proposed B65 would be rerouted to travel on Atlantic Av in both directions between Downtown Brooklyn and Washington Av, using a wider street as compared to Dean St/Bergen St and providing a direct connection to the Atlantic Av-Barclays Center subway and LIRR stations. This would also remove a difficult right turn from Bergen St onto Smith St, reducing delays. Existing B65 service on Dean St and Bergen St west of Washington Av would be discontinued. The proposed B65 would also be slightly shortened on its eastern end, terminating at St Marks Av/ Bergen St and Ralph Av. Service on Ralph Av would be provided by the proposed B15 and the proposed B45. Service on Buffalo Av would be discontinued.
[p273_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p273_c0023] Frequencies would be decreased during Weekday Late Evenings and on Saturdays and Sundays but would still match ridership demand. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p273_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p273_c0025] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p273_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p273_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p273_c0028]

[p277_c0022] East New York
### [p277_c0023] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p277_c0024] The proposed B66 would be a new route connecting Williamsburg and Broadway Junction. This route would replace existing B60 service between Williamsburg Bridge Plaza and Fulton St, with an extension to the future accessible Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L station. Northbound service between Broadway and Wilson Av would be rerouted from narrow Decatur St onto Cooper St to match southbound service, simplifying the route path. Northbound service between Johnson Av and Meserole St would be rerouted from Graham Av onto Bushwick Av to match southbound service and to provide a direct connection to the Montrose Av L station and to the proposed B15.
[p277_c0025] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p277_c0026] Frequencies would be slightly lower compared to the existing B60 but would still match ridership demand. The route would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p277_c0027] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p277_c0028] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p277_c0029] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p277_c0030] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p277_c0031]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 15 20 14 17 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 AM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 29 23 19 24 29 SUNDAY 24 hours 30 27 23 30 30
```
[p277_c0032] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 280

### [p280_c0001] Local
### [p280_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p280_c0003] B67
### [p280_c0004] Kensington - Vinegar Hill
[p280_c0005] Related Routes: B67, B57, B62
### [p280_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p280_c0007] Existing: 7.0 miles Proposed: 5.7 miles
### [p280_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p280_c0009] Existing: 911 feet Proposed: 950 feet
### [p280_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p280_c0011] Existing: 3.4 per mile Proposed: 1.9 per mile
### [p280_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p280_c0013] Bus:
[p280_c0014] B16, B25, B26, B27, B35, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B45, B48, B52, B54, B55, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B69, B81
### [p280_c0015] Subway:
[p280_c0016] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G
### [p280_c0017] LIRR:
[p280_c0018] Atlantic Terminal
### [p280_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p280_c0020] The proposed B67's northern terminal would be at Sands St/Navy St in Vinegar Hill at all times the route operates, which would be an extension during weekday evenings and on weekends. Service through the Brooklyn Navy Yard to South Williamsburg would be discontinued. The connection between the Navy Yard, Williamsburg, and points north would instead be provided by the proposed B62 LTD on Flushing Av at increased frequencies.
### [p280_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p280_c0022] • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p280_c0023] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p280_c0024] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p280_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p280_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p280_c0027]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 12 22 11 21 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 30 30 30 30 30 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 30 30 30 30 30
```
[p280_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

[p284_c0006] Related Routes: B68, B1, B49
### [p284_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p284_c0008] Existing: 7.6 miles Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p284_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p284_c0010] Existing: 786 feet Proposed: 955 feet
### [p284_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p284_c0012] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.6 per mile
### [p284_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p284_c0014] Bus:
[p284_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B11, B16, B31, B35, B36, B44 SBS, B49, B55, B61, B81, B82, B82, B100
### [p284_c0016] Subway:
[p284_c0017] B Q F G
### [p284_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p284_c0019] The proposed B68 would mostly maintain its existing routing but would have a new southern terminal at Kingsborough Community College. Instead of traveling west from Coney Island Av to the Coney Island-Stillwell Av D/F/N/Q station, it would travel east to the college via Brighton Beach Av and Oriental Blvd along with the B1. This would simplify service to the college and would allow for better management of overcrowding, as both bus routes to the college would serve the same Brighton Beach B/Q subway station. The proposed B68 would provide similar bus connections to the B49 north of Brighton Beach Av. Service between Coney Island Av and Stillwell Av via Brighton Beach Av, W 5th St, and Neptune Av would be provided by the proposed B49.
[p284_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p284_c0021] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time. Service spans would be slightly shortened overnight, with new 24-hour service provided on the proposed B49 instead.
### [p284_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p284_c0023] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor

### [p288_c0001] Local

### [p288_c0003] B69
### [p288_c0004] Greenpoint - Windsor Terrace
[p288_c0005] Related Routes: B69, B48, B62, B67, B81
### [p288_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p288_c0007] Existing: 6.2 miles Proposed: 7.6 miles
### [p288_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p288_c0009] Existing: 748 feet Proposed: 1,002 feet
### [p288_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p288_c0011] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.8 per mile
### [p288_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p288_c0013] Bus:
[p288_c0014] B25, B26, B38, B41, B41 SBS, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B62, B65, B66, B67, B81, Q54, Q59, Q68
### [p288_c0015] Subway:
[p288_c0016] 2 3 B Q C F G L
### [p288_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p288_c0018] The proposed B69 would be rerouted on its northern end to Greenpoint via the existing B48 travel path, maintaining its connection to much of the Brooklyn Navy Yard while improving connectivity in northern Brooklyn. Connections would now be available to the future accessible Lorimer St J/M and L stations. Service on the western portion of Flushing Av would be provided by the proposed B48 and B62. Service between Park Slope and the western end of the Brooklyn Navy Yard would still be available via the proposed B67. On its southern end, the proposed B69 would be shortened, terminating at Prospect Park W/20 St. Service on McDonald Av would be provided by the proposed B67 and B81.
[p288_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p288_c0020] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time. Service spans would be lengthened.
### [p288_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p288_c0022] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor

### [p293_c0001] Local
### [p293_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p293_c0003] B70 Sunset Park - Dyker Heights Related Routes: B70, B35
### [p293_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p293_c0005] Existing: 4.9 miles Proposed: 4.7 miles
### [p293_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p293_c0007] Existing: 726 feet Proposed: 1,043 feet
### [p293_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p293_c0009] Existing: 2.4 per mile Proposed: 2.5 per mile
### [p293_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p293_c0011] Bus:
[p293_c0012] B1, B4, B8, B9, B11, B16, B35, B37, B63, B64, S53, S79 SBS, S93
### [p293_c0013] Subway:
[p293_c0014] D N R
### [p293_c0015] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p293_c0016] The proposed B70 would mostly maintain its existing routing except for a new northern terminal at Industry City. Instead of terminating at 39 St/1 Av, the proposed route would terminate at 3 Av/30 St, providing a new connection to the retail area from southwest Brooklyn and from the future accessible 36 St D/N/R station. Service on 39 St west of 3 Av would continue to be provided by the proposed B35.
[p293_c0017] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p293_c0018] Frequencies would be slightly increased during Weekday Middays. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p293_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p293_c0020] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access
### [p293_c0021] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p293_c0022] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p293_c0023]


### [p298_c0005] Draft Plan | 297
> [p298_c0006] The map illustrates the B74 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically the Mermaid Avenue section. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating added service. It starts at the Coney Island station (marked with a subway icon) and travels west along Mermaid Avenue. It then turns north onto W 33 St, then west onto W 37 St, and finally south onto Bayview Avenue. The map also shows the existing bus network (grey lines) and discontinued service (black dashed lines). Key landmarks include Calvert Vaux Park, Coney Island Boat Basin, Kaiser Park, and the W 8 St - NY Aquarium. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (black dashed line), Proposed Bus Network (grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with cross), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange area). A scale bar at the bottom left shows distances up to 0.5 miles, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom right corner shows the location of the area within the greater New York City region.

## Page 300

### [p300_c0001] Local
### [p300_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p300_c0003] B76
### [p300_c0004] Canarsie - Broadway Junction
[p300_c0005] Related Routes: B17, B42, B60
### [p300_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p300_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0008] Proposed: 4.7 miles
### [p300_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p300_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0011] Proposed: 1,015 feet
### [p300_c0012] TURNS PER MILE
[p300_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0014] Proposed: 1.2 per mile
### [p300_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p300_c0016] Bus:
[p300_c0017] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B10, B12, B14, B15, B17, B20, B25, B35, B42, B53, B55, B60, B66, B82, B82 SBS, B83, B103, Q24, Q56
### [p300_c0018] Subway:
[p300_c0019] 3 A C J Z L
### [p300_c0020] LIRR:
[p300_c0021] East New York
### [p300_c0022] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p300_c0023] The proposed B76 would be a new route connecting the Paerdegat section of Canarsie with Broadway Junction, replacing the existing part-time B17 Paerdegat branch. The proposed new B76 would connect E 80 St/Paerdegat 15 St and the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station in the peak hours, traveling via Avenue L and Rockaway Pkwy, and continuing via Rockaway Av to the future accessible Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L station. The route would remove turns between Paerdegat and the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station, reducing delays. The route would also provide additional service during the peaks to the B42 on Rockaway Pkwy and to the B60 on Rockaway Av.
[p300_c0024] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p300_c0025] Frequencies would be similar to the existing B17 Paerdegat branch. Spans would be reduced as compared to the existing B17 Paerdegat branch.
### [p300_c0026] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p300_c0027] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p300_c0028] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p300_c0029] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p300_c0030]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 6:00 AM - 9:00 AM 3:00 PM - 8:00 PM 10 - 10 12 - Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY N/A - - - - - SUNDAY N/A - - - - -
```

## Page 303

### [p303_c0001] Local
### [p303_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p303_c0003] B81
### [p303_c0004] Midwood - Red Hook
[p303_c0005] Related Routes: B57, B61, B67, B69, B103
### [p303_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p303_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0008] Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p303_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p303_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0011] Proposed: 995 feet
### [p303_c0012] URNS PER MILE
[p303_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0014] Proposed: 2.2 per mile
### [p303_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p303_c0016] Bus:
[p303_c0017] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B11, B16, B27, B35, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B49, B55, B61, B63, B67, B68, B69, B103, Q35
### [p303_c0018] Subway:
[p303_c0019] 2 5 F G R Q
### [p303_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p303_c0021] The proposed B81 would be a new route connecting Red Hook and Midwood, providing new direct service from Red Hook and Park Slope to central Brooklyn and providing new connections to the Q and 2/5 subway lines as well as high-frequency bus routes such as the B8, B35, B41, and B44. The proposed B81 would follow the B61 travel path from the Red Hook ferry terminal to Prospect Park W/20 St, replacing the shortened B61 on Prospect Park W between Bartel-Pritchard Sq and 20 St. (In Red Hook, the Red Hook-bound B81 would travel via Columbia St for direct service to IKEA, while the proposed B61 would travel directly from Dwight St to Van Dyke St.) In Windsor Terrace and Kensington, the proposed B81 would follow the B69 travel path on McDonald Av, replacing the shortened B69. East of McDonald Av, the proposed B81 would travel via Cortelyou Rd and Flatbush Av, replacing the shortened B103 LTD travel path east of Coney Island Av and providing new service on Cortelyou Rd west of Coney Island Av.
[p303_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p303_c0023] Frequencies and service spans would be similar to existing B103 LTD trips between Midwood and Downtown Brooklyn.
### [p303_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p303_c0025] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p303_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p303_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

## Page 307

### [p307_c0001] Local
### [p307_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p307_c0003] B82
### [p307_c0004] Kings Highway / Flatlands Avenue
[p307_c0005] Service between Gravesend and Spring Creek Towers
[p307_c0006] Related Routes: B82, B6, B64, B82 SBS
### [p307_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p307_c0008] Existing: 10.5 miles Proposed: 9.4 miles
### [p307_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p307_c0010] Existing: 746 feet Proposed: 1,088 feet
### [p307_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p307_c0012] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p307_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p307_c0014] Bus:
[p307_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B17, B31, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B64, B68, B76, B82 SBS, B83, B100, B103, Q35
### [p307_c0016] Subway:
[p307_c0017] B Q D N F L
### [p307_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p307_c0019] The proposed B82 would be shortened on its southern end, terminating at Cropsey Av/Bay 37 St as the B82 SBS does. The Gravesend-bound B82 would serve Shore Pkwy between Bay Pkwy and 26 Av, replacing existing B6 service. Service on Cropsey Av between 26 Av and Coney Island would be provided by the proposed B64. In Flatlands, the proposed B82 would travel between Kings Hwy and Flatlands Av via Avenue K instead of via Flatbush Av, sharing the same travel path as the B82 SBS, traveling on the Kings Hwy bus lanes for a greater distance, and avoiding congestion on Flatbush Av. Existing service on Flatlands Av between Flatbush Av and Avenue K would be discontinued.
[p307_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p307_c0021] Frequencies would be slightly increased during Early Evenings on weekends. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p307_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p307_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p307_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p307_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p307_c0026]


### [p312_c0004] Kings Highway / Flatlands Avenue

[p312_c0006] Related Routes: B82 SBS

### [p312_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING

### [p312_c0014] Bus:

[p312_c0017] B Q D N F L
### [p312_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p312_c0019] The proposed B82 SBS would maintain its existing routing.
[p312_c0020] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p312_c0021] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays to 10-minutes-or-better. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p312_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p312_c0023] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor

## Page 315

### [p315_c0001] Local
### [p315_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p315_c0003] B83
### [p315_c0004] Pennsylvania Avenue
[p315_c0005] Service between Broadway Junction and Gateway Center
[p315_c0006] Related Routes: B83, B5, B6 LTD, B15, B20, B55, B103
### [p315_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p315_c0008] Existing: 5.3 miles Proposed: 4.8 miles
### [p315_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p315_c0010] Existing: 1,015 feet Proposed: 1,369 feet
### [p315_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p315_c0012] Existing: 1.9 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p315_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p315_c0014] Bus:
[p315_c0015] B53, B55, B60, B66, B76, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q24, Q51, Q56
### [p315_c0016] Subway:
[p315_c0017] 3 A C J Z L
### [p315_c0018] LIRR:
[p315_c0019] East New York
### [p315_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p315_c0021] The proposed B83 would be streamlined to stay on Pennsylvania Av, providing quicker trips between Spring Creek Towers and Gateway Center, and subway connections at the Pennsylvania Av 3 and Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L stations. This reroute would remove turns and reduce delays. Existing service on Vandalia Av and Van Siclen Av would be discontinued, though similar subway connections would be available via the proposed B6 LTD, B5, and B103 on Cozine Av, along with the proposed B20 on Stanley Av, and the B15 and B55 on New Lots Av.
[p315_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p315_c0023] Frequencies would be increased during Middays, PM Peak, and Early Evenings on weekdays, as well as during Saturday mornings and Sunday late evenings.
[p315_c0024] Service would now operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends, though new proposed overnight trips would only operate between Broadway Junction and Spring Creek Towers.
### [p315_c0025] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p315_c0026] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p315_c0027] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p315_c0028] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p315_c0029]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 8 8 8 17 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 15 12 10 11 18 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 15 15 15 18
```

## Page 319

### [p319_c0001] Local
### [p319_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p319_c0003] B100 Mill Basin - Midwood Related Routes: B100, B2, B31
### [p319_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p319_c0005] Existing: 4.3 miles Proposed: 4.3 miles
### [p319_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p319_c0007] Existing: 819 feet Proposed: 1,086 feet
### [p319_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p319_c0009] Existing: 3.0 per mile Proposed: 2.7 per mile
### [p319_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p319_c0011] Bus:
[p319_c0012] B3, B7, B9, B31, B41, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B47, B49, B82, B82 SBS
### [p319_c0013] Subway:
[p319_c0014] B Q
### [p319_c0015] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p319_c0016] The proposed B100 would mostly maintain its existing routing, except for using Avenue R instead of Quentin Rd between E 16 St and Gerritsen Av in order to avoid congestion along Kings Hwy and to replace the B2 which is proposed to be retired. Service on Quentin Rd would be discontinued.
[p319_c0017] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p319_c0018] Frequencies would be increased Saturday Late Evenings and all day on Sundays. Frequencies would be slightly decreased Weekday Late Evenings. Service span would be increased.
### [p319_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p319_c0020] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency
### [p319_c0021] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p319_c0022] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

## Page 323

### [p323_c0001] Limited
### [p323_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p323_c0003] B103 East New York - Midwood Related Routes: B103, B6, B37, B41, B41 XT, B81
### [p323_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p323_c0005] Existing: 11.7 miles Proposed: 6.4 miles
### [p323_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p323_c0007] Existing: 1,693 feet Proposed: 1,600 feet
### [p323_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p323_c0009] Existing: 1.6 per mile Proposed: 1.6 per mile
### [p323_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p323_c0011] Bus:
[p323_c0012] B5, B6, B7, B11, B15, B17, B20, B40, B41, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B55, B60, B76, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B83, Q8, Q35
### [p323_c0013] Subway:
[p323_c0014] 2 3 5
### [p323_c0015] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p323_c0016] On its eastern end, the proposed B103 would be extended to the future accessible New Lots Av 3 station via Glenwood Rd, Cozine Av, and Ashford St, providing a new connection between Canarsie and East New York. This extension would also shorten the walking connection between the B103 and the E 105 St L station. On its western end, the proposed B103 would be shortened, with all trips terminating at The Junction at Flatbush Av/Nostrand Av. Service between The Junction and Downtown Brooklyn would be provided by both the proposed B41 Local and B41 Crosstown, as well as the 2/5 subways. Service between Cortelyou Rd and The Junction would be provided by the proposed B81. Service on 3 Av would be provided by the proposed B37, service on 4 Av would be discontinued.
[p323_c0017] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p323_c0018] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p323_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p323_c0020] • New connections • Fewer route patterns • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p323_c0021] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p323_c0022] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p323_c0023]

### [p327_c0001] Local
[p327_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p327_c0003] Q24
### [p327_c0004] Atlantic Avenue
[p327_c0005] Service between Broadway Junction and Jamaica
[p327_c0006] Related Routes: Q24, B53, Q42
### [p327_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p327_c0008] Existing: 8.4 miles Proposed: 6.5 miles
### [p327_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p327_c0010] Existing: 689 feet Proposed: 1,129 feet
### [p327_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p327_c0012] Existing: 1.0 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p327_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p327_c0014] Bus:
[p327_c0015] B53, B60, B66, B76, B83, Q1, Q7, Q8, Q9, Q10, Q21, Q31, Q37, Q43, Q44 SBS, Q45, Q54, Q55, Q56, Q57, Q60, Q65, Q75, Q114
### [p327_c0016] Subway:
[p327_c0017] A C E F J Z L
### [p327_c0018] LIRR:
[p327_c0019] Jamaica, East New York
### [p327_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p327_c0021] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed Q24 would be shortened on its western end to Broadway Junction and realigned in Jamaica (Queens) to improve route reliability. Service on the discontinued portion of Broadway would be provided by the proposed B53. In Jamaica, the route would no longer serve Jamaica Hospital and would terminate on 89 Av/Parsons Blvd instead of Archer Av/Merrick Blvd. Service to Jamaica Hospital would be provided by the proposed Q42.
[p327_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p327_c0023] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p327_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p327_c0025] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p327_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p327_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p327_c0028]


### [p332_c0003] Q54
### [p332_c0004] Metropolitan Avenue
[p332_c0005] Service between Williamsburg and Jamaica
[p332_c0006] Related Routes: Q54
### [p332_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p332_c0008] Existing: 9.7 miles Proposed: 9.8 miles
### [p332_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p332_c0010] Existing: 768 feet Proposed: 1,443 feet

[p332_c0012] Existing: 0.9 per mile Proposed: 0.5 per mile
### [p332_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p332_c0014] Bus:
[p332_c0015] B53, B57, B62, B66, B69, Q1, Q8, Q9, Q10, Q14, Q21, Q24, Q25, Q37, Q38, Q40, Q42, Q44 SBS, Q47, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q55, Q56, Q57, Q58, Q59, Q60, Q65, Q67, Q68, Q73, Q80, Q98
### [p332_c0016] Subway:
[p332_c0017] E G L J M Z
### [p332_c0018] LIRR:
[p332_c0019] Jamaica
### [p332_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p332_c0021] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed Q54 would maintain its existing routing with a minor realignment from Metropolitan Av to Hillside Av approaching Jamaica in Queens.
[p332_c0022] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p332_c0023] Frequencies would be increased Weekday Middays and Early Evenings to provide all-day frequent service between Brooklyn and Queens. Service would still operate 24 hours.
### [p332_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p332_c0025] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency
### [p332_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p332_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

### [p337_c0004] Myrtle Avenue
[p337_c0005] Service between Ridgewood and Jamaica

[p337_c0008] Existing: 4.4 miles Proposed: 6.9 miles

[p337_c0010] Existing: 703 feet Proposed: 1,231 feet

### [p337_c0016] Subway:
[p337_c0017] E J M Z L
### [p337_c0018] LIRR:
[p337_c0019] Jamaica
### [p337_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p337_c0021] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed Q55 would maintain its existing routing, but would be extended east in Queens from its existing terminal in Richmond Hill to Jamaica via Jamaica Av. This extension would provide better connectivity between Brooklyn and Queens, improving interborough service.
[p337_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p337_c0023] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p337_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p337_c0025] • Interborough route • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access
### [p337_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p337_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p337_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 15 10 11 16 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 17 15 13 13 23 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 20 20 20 25
```
[p337_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 345

### [p345_c0001] Local
[p345_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p345_c0003] Q59
### [p345_c0004] Grand Avenue / Grand Street
[p345_c0005] Service between Williamsburg and Rego Park
[p345_c0006] Related Routes: Q59, B53, B62, Q54, Q68
### [p345_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p345_c0008] Existing: 7.2 miles Proposed: 6.0 miles
### [p345_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p345_c0010] Existing: 828 feet Proposed: 1,171 feet
### [p345_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p345_c0012] Existing: 1.9 per mile Proposed: 0.9 per mile
### [p345_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p345_c0014] Bus:
[p345_c0015] B53, B57, B62, B66, B69, Q14, Q18, Q21, Q38, Q39, Q47, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q54, Q58, Q60, Q63, Q67, Q68, Q80, Q98
### [p345_c0016] Subway:
[p345_c0017] E G J M Z L
### [p345_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p345_c0019] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed Q59 would mostly maintain its existing routing, but would be streamlined at both ends. On its western end, the route would travel directly to Williamsburg Bridge Plaza instead of looping around Metropolitan Av, Grand St, Kent/Wythe Avs, and Broadway. This would provide faster, more direct routing and focus resources on the Grand St corridor. Service on Kent/Wythe Avs and Broadway would be provided by the proposed B53, with an increase in frequency as compared to the existing B32. Service on Metropolitan Av and western Grand St would be discontinued, but alternative service would be available via the proposed B62 or the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q68. On its eastern end in Queens, the route would not divert off of Queens Blvd. Queens-bound in East Williamsburg, the proposed Q59 would use Stewart Av instead of Gardner Av to connect Metropolitan Av to Grand St, avoiding a difficult turn.
[p345_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p345_c0021] Frequencies would be increased Weekday Middays and Early Evenings. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p345_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p345_c0023] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets
### [p345_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p345_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p345_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 11 15 12 12 23 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 20 18 13 12 23 SUNDAY 24 hours 30 23 13 16 30
```
[p345_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 349

### [p349_c0001] Limited
### [p349_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p349_c0003] Q8
### [p349_c0004] 101st Avenue
[p349_c0005] Service between East New York and Jamaica
[p349_c0006] Related Routes: Q8, B13, Q51
### [p349_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p349_c0008] Existing: 8.2 miles Proposed: 6.7 miles
### [p349_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p349_c0010] Existing: 920 feet Proposed: 1,324 feet
### [p349_c0011] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p349_c0012] Bus:
[p349_c0013] B5, B6 LTD, B13, B14, B15, B55, Q1, Q2, Q3, Q9, Q10, Q21, Q24, Q25, Q31, Q37, Q40, Q42, Q43, Q44 SBS, Q45, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q54, Q55, Q56, Q57, Q60, Q65, Q75, Q76, Q77, Q82, Q83, Q84, Q86, Q109, Q115
### [p349_c0014] Subway:
[p349_c0015] 3 A C E F J Z
### [p349_c0016] LIRR:
[p349_c0017] Jamaica
### [p349_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p349_c0019] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q8 would maintain mostly the same routing, but would be shortened and rerouted at its western end, serving the future accessible New Lots Av 3 station instead of Gateway Center. This would improve Brooklyn-Queens interborough service, providing a more direct connection to East New York and the 3 train with fewer turns. Service along Fountain Av to Gateway Center would still be provided by the proposed B13. Service between Gateway Center and Queens would be provided by the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q51.
### [p349_c0020] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p349_c0021] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
[p349_c0022] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p349_c0023] Weekday frequencies would be slightly increased to 10-minutes-or-better all-day and service would now be provided 24 hours.
### [p349_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p349_c0025]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing Proposed 4:30 AM - 12:30 AM 24 hours 8 or better 8 or better 15 or better 10 or better SATURDAY Existing Proposed 5:30 AM - 12:30 AM 24 hours 20 or better 20 or better 15 or better 15 or better SUNDAY Existing Proposed 5:30 AM - 12:30 AM 24 hours 30 or better 30 or better 20 or better 20 or better
```
[p349_c0026] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p349_c0027] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 353

### [p353_c0001] Rush
### [p353_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p353_c0003] Q35
### [p353_c0004] Midwood - Rockaway Park
[p353_c0005] Related Routes: Q35, Q22, B41
### [p353_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p353_c0007] Existing: 8.5 miles Proposed: 9.4 miles
### [p353_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p353_c0009] Existing: 898 feet Proposed: 1,266 feet
### [p353_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p353_c0011] Bus:
[p353_c0012] B3, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B11, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q22, Q53 SBS
### [p353_c0013] Subway:
[p353_c0014] 2 5 S
### [p353_c0015] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p353_c0016] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q35 Rush would be rerouted in the Rockaways from Newport Av to Rockaway Beach Blvd, replacing Q22 service in that segment and improving speed and reliability. The route would also be extended east from Beach 116 St to Beach 108 St to provide connections to the ferry, Scholars' Academy, and other destinations.
[p353_c0017] As a new "Rush" route with a local and non-stop portion, stops along Rockaway Beach Blvd would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing, while stops along Flatbush Av would only be located at key destinations and major transfer points.
[p353_c0018] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p353_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p353_c0020] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p353_c0021] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p353_c0022]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing 24 hours 12 or better 20 or better Proposed 24 hours 12 or better 20 or better SATURDAY Existing 24 hours 30 or better 15 or better Proposed 24 hours 30 or better 15 or better SUNDAY Existing 24 hours 20 or better 20 or better Proposed 24 hours 20 or better 20 or better
```
[p353_c0023] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p353_c0024] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

> [p354_c0006] The map illustrates the Q35 bus route in the Midwood and Rockaway Park areas. The route is primarily shown as a thick purple line, indicating 'Existing Service'. A green line segment along Flatbush Av is labeled 'B41', with a purple box stating 'Underlying local service in non-stop segment would be provided by the proposed B41.' The route follows Flatbush Av, then turns south onto the Maine Pkwy Bridge, and continues along Rockaway Beach Blvd. Key landmarks include Holy Cross Cemetery, Rockaway Park, Starrett City, Canarsie Park, Mill Basin, Bergen Beach, Marine Park, Floyd Bennett Field, Plum Beach, and Manhattan Beach Park. The map also shows subway lines, accessible stations, future accessible stations, LIRR tracks, and institutional land uses. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols used. ## Page 357

### [p357_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p357_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p357_c0003] Q51
### [p357_c0004] Gateway Center - Cambria Heights
### [p357_c0005] ROUTE LENGTH
[p357_c0006] Existing: N/A
[p357_c0007] Proposed: 9.3 miles
### [p357_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p357_c0009] Existing: N/A
[p357_c0010] Proposed: 3,050 feet
### [p357_c0011] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p357_c0012] Bus:
[p357_c0013] B5, B13, B83, Q1, Q3, Q4, Q5, Q7, Q9, Q10, Q21, Q25, Q26, Q37, Q40, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q77, Q78, Q84, Q85, Q86, Q109, Q111, Q114, Q115
### [p357_c0014] Subway:
[p357_c0015] A
### [p357_c0016] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p357_c0017] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q51 would be a new interborough route connecting Gateway Center to Cambria Heights, Queens via Linden Blvd. This new east-west service in southeast Queens would connect with over 25 bus routes as well as the A train at Rockaway Blvd, providing new access across the borough without having to travel to downtown Jamaica to transfer to another route.
[p357_c0018] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p357_c0019] Since this is a new route, weekday frequencies would initially be 10 minutes-or-better during peak hours and 15 minutes-or-better in the off-peak hours.
### [p357_c0020] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p357_c0021] • Interborough route • New connections • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p357_c0022] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p357_c0023]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 10 or better 15 or better SATURDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 30 or better 30 or better SUNDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 30 or better 30 or better
```
[p357_c0024] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p357_c0025] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 360

### [p360_c0001] Limited
### [p360_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p360_c0003] Q58
### [p360_c0004] Ridgewood - Flushing via Corona
[p360_c0005] Related Routes: Q58, Q23, Q98
### [p360_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p360_c0007] Existing: 8.2 miles Proposed: 8.1 miles
### [p360_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p360_c0009] Existing: 777 feet Proposed: 1,574 feet
### [p360_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p360_c0011] Bus:
[p360_c0012] B7, B13, B26, B38, B52, B54, B57, Q14, Q16, Q18, Q19, Q20, Q23, Q38, Q39, Q44 SBS, Q47, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q54, Q55, Q59, Q60, Q63, Q67, Q72, Q80, Q98
### [p360_c0013] Subway:
[p360_c0014] 7 R M L
### [p360_c0015] LIRR:
[p360_c0016] Mets - Willets Point, Flushing
### [p360_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p360_c0018] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the Q58 would mostly maintain its existing routing, but would be rerouted at its northern end to serve 111 St and Roosevelt Av instead of 108 St and Horace Harding Expwy, creating a more direct travel path into Flushing and connections to new destinations. The proposed Q58 changes are related to the proposed Q98, which would be a faster alternative to the Q58. The proposed Q98 would provide an even more direct travel path into Flushing, operating nonstop from Queens Center Mall to Flushing via Queens Blvd, Horace Harding Expwy, and College Point Blvd. Service along 108 St would continue to be provided by the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q23.
[p360_c0019] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p360_c0020] Frequencies would be slightly decreased and reallocated to the proposed new Q98. Service would still operate 24 hours.
### [p360_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p360_c0022] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p360_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p360_c0024]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing 24 hours 4 or better 7 or better Proposed 24 hours 6 or better 8 or better SATURDAY Existing 24 hours 5 or better 7 or better Proposed 24 hours 8 or better 10 or better SUNDAY Existing 24 hours 10 or better 9 or better Proposed 24 hours 10 or better 10 or better
```
[p360_c0025] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p360_c0026] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 365

### [p365_c0001] Local
### [p365_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p365_c0003] Q68 Williamsburg - Steinway
[p365_c0004] Related Routes: B24, Q101
### [p365_c0005] ROUTE LENGTH
[p365_c0006] Existing: N/A
[p365_c0007] Proposed: 7.6 miles
### [p365_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p365_c0009] Existing: N/A
[p365_c0010] Proposed: 1,167 feet
### [p365_c0011] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p365_c0012] Bus:
[p365_c0013] B39, B43, B44 SBS, B53, B62, B66, B69, Q18, Q19, Q32, Q39, Q54, Q59, Q60, Q63, Q66, Q67, Q69, Q104, Q105
### [p365_c0014] Subway:
[p365_c0015] 7 R G J M Z L
### [p365_c0016] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p365_c0017] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q68 would be a new interborough route directly connecting Williamsburg with Steinway and Astoria in Queens. The route would replace the southern half of the existing B24 in Williamsburg as well as the existing Q101 along Steinway St. This new service improves Brooklyn-Queens connectivity, addressing customer requests for better interborough service. Existing Q101 service to Manhattan would be discontinued, however Manhattan-bound service would still be available through transfers to the proposed Q32 or Q60.
[p365_c0018] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p365_c0019] Frequencies would be increased as compared to the existing B24 and service would operate 24 hours.
### [p365_c0020] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p365_c0021] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p365_c0022] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p365_c0023]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 15 or better 30 or better SATURDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 30 or better 30 or better SUNDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 30 or better 30 or better
```
[p365_c0024] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p365_c0025] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 369

### [p369_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
[p369_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p369_c0003] Q98
### [p369_c0004] Ridgewood - Flushing via Horace Harding Expy
[p369_c0005] Related Routes: Q58, Q58 LTD
### [p369_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p369_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p369_c0008] Proposed: 7.7 miles
### [p369_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p369_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p369_c0011] Proposed: 2,704 feet
### [p369_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p369_c0013] Bus:
[p369_c0014] B7, B13, B26, B38, B52, B54, B57, Q14, Q16, Q18, Q20, Q21, Q23, Q38, Q39, Q44 SBS, Q47, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q54, Q55, Q58, Q59, Q60, Q63, Q67, Q80
### [p369_c0015] Subway:
[p369_c0016] 7 R M L
### [p369_c0017] LIRR:
[p369_c0018] Flushing
### [p369_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p369_c0020] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q98 would be a new, faster alternative to the proposed Q58. The proposed Q98 would provide a more direct travel path from Ridgewood to Flushing via Queens Blvd, Horace Harding Expy and College Point Blvd, serving only key destinations and transfer points. Additionally, the route would operate mostly nonstop from Queens Center Mall to Flushing, while the proposed Q58 would continue to serve stops along Corona Av.
### [p369_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p369_c0022] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
[p369_c0023] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p369_c0024] Weekday frequency would be 10 minutes-or-better and service would operate 24 hours.
### [p369_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p369_c0026]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 10 or better 10 or better SATURDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 15 or better 15 or better SUNDAY Existing – – – Proposed 24 hours 15 or better 15 or better
```
[p369_c0027] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p369_c0028] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 372

### [p372_c0001] Local
### [p372_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p372_c0003] Q109 109th Avenue Service between East New York and Jamaica Related Routes: Q7, Q41, Q112
### [p372_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p372_c0005] Existing: N/A Proposed: 6.2 miles
### [p372_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p372_c0007] Existing: N/A Proposed: 1,298 feet
### [p372_c0008] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p372_c0009] Bus:
[p372_c0010] B13, B14, Q1, Q7, Q8, Q9, Q10, Q11, Q21, Q25, Q37, Q40, Q42, Q44 SBS, Q51, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q57, Q60, Q65, Q111, Q114, Q115
### [p372_c0011] Subway:
[p372_c0012] A C E F J Z
### [p372_c0013] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p372_c0014] As part of the Queens Bus Network Redesign, the proposed Q109 would be a new route serving segments of the existing Q7, Q41, and Q112 along Pitkin Av, Sutter Av, Rockaway Blvd, 109 Av, and Tuskegee Airmen Wy from East New York to Jamaica, Queens. This new route would provide a more direct east-west path and a new interborough connection from Brooklyn to Queens. The Q109 would replace existing Q41 service and part of the existing Q7 along Sutter Av and Pitkin Av. Service to Howard Beach would still be provided by the proposed Q21.
[p372_c0015] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p372_c0016] Frequencies and spans would be similar to the existing Q41.
### [p372_c0017] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p372_c0018] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p372_c0019] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN
[p372_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Peak Frequency* Off-Peak Frequency* WEEKDAY Existing — — — Proposed 5:00 AM - 1:00 AM 9 or better 15 or better SATURDAY Existing — — — Proposed 5:30 AM - 1:00 AM 30 or better 30 or better SUNDAY Existing — — — Proposed 6:30 AM - 1:00 AM 30 or better 60 or better
```
[p372_c0021] * Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency during the AM and PM peak periods (6-9 AM and 4-7 PM).
[p372_c0022] Off-Peak Frequency represents the minimum frequency at any point during the day (between 6 AM and 9 PM).

## Page 375

### [p375_c0001] Express
### [p375_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p375_c0003] BM1
### [p375_c0004] Mill Basin - Downtown Manhattan
[p375_c0005] Related Routes: BM1, BM7, BM8, M55
### [p375_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p375_c0007] Existing: 14.7 miles Proposed: 13.7 miles
### [p375_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p375_c0009] Existing: 1,274 feet Proposed: 1,577 feet
### [p375_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p375_c0011] Existing: 1.5 per mile Proposed: 1.4 per mile
### [p375_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p375_c0013] The proposed BM1 would refer only to the Downtown peak-only variant of the existing BM1. The proposed BM1 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Flatbush Av and Ocean Av, the BM1 would use Avenue J instead of Avenue K in order to avoid a difficult series of turns at Avenue K/Flatbush Av. Existing service would be discontinued on this portion of Avenue K. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM1 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. In Manhattan, the proposed BM1 would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7, BM8, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
[p375_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p375_c0015] Inbound frequencies and span would remain the same as the existing BM1 Downtown variant. Outbound frequencies would be slightly reduced and the span shortened due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p375_c0016] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p375_c0017] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p375_c0018] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p375_c0019] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p375_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:00 AM - 9:00 AM - 23 - - - Outbound 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM - - 60 30 -
```
[p375_c0021] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p375_c0022] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 380

### [p380_c0001] Express
### [p380_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p380_c0003] BM1c Mill Basin - Downtown/Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM1, BM7c, BM8c, M55
### [p380_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p380_c0005] Existing: 19.6 miles Proposed: 19.4 miles
### [p380_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p380_c0007] Existing: 1,319 feet Proposed: 1,617 feet
### [p380_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p380_c0009] Existing: 1.1 per mile Proposed: 1.1 per mile
### [p380_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p380_c0011] The proposed BM1c would refer to the Downtown/Midtown off-peak "combo" variant of the existing BM1. The proposed BM1c would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Flatbush Av and Ocean Av, the BM1c would use Avenue J instead of Avenue K in order to avoid a difficult series of turns at Avenue K/Flatbush Av. Existing service would be discontinued on this portion of Avenue K. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM1c would use Beverly Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. In Manhattan, the proposed BM1c would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7c, BM8c, the M55, and numerous subway lines.
### [p380_c0012] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p380_c0013] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
[p380_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p380_c0015] Weekday spans would be shortened and outbound frequencies reduced due to low ridership on some trips. Saturday service would be discontinued due to low ridership.
### [p380_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p380_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p380_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 5:00 AM - 7:00 AM 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM 60 60 60 - - Outbound 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM - - - - 60
```
[p380_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p380_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

> [p381_c0007] The map illustrates the BM1c bus route in Manhattan, connecting Mill Basin to Downtown/Midtown. The route is shown with a thick green line for 'Added Service' and a dashed black line for 'Discontinued Service'. The route starts at E66 St in Mill Basin, travels north on Veterans Av, then west on Avenue K, then south on Avenue J, then east on Ocean Av, then north on Cortelyou Rd, then west on Beverly Rd, then north on Prospect Expwy, and finally east on McDonald Av to Fort Hamilton Pkwy. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' as thin grey lines, 'Subway' as blue lines with circles, 'Accessible Stations' as grey circles, 'Future Accessible Stations' as black circles, 'LIRR' as grey squares, and 'Institutional Land Uses' as orange shaded areas. Key landmarks include Green-Wood Cemetery, Parkade Av, Prospect Park, Fort Hamilton Pkwy, Sunset Park, Borough Park, Midwood, Flatbush, Flatlands, Marine Park, and Mill Basin. A legend in the bottom right corner defines the symbols. A scale bar shows 0, 0.5, and 1 Miles. A north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left shows the location of the study area within the New York City metropolitan region.

## Page 385

### [p385_c0001] Express
### [p385_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p385_c0003] BM2
### [p385_c0004] Spring Creek/Canarsie - Downtown Manhattan
[p385_c0005] Related Routes: BM2, BM7, BM8, M55
### [p385_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p385_c0007] Existing: 15.5 miles Proposed: 14.6 miles
### [p385_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p385_c0009] Existing: 1,373 feet Proposed: 1,722 feet
### [p385_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p385_c0011] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.1 per mile
### [p385_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p385_c0013] The proposed BM2 would refer only to the Downtown peak-only variant of the existing BM2. The proposed BM2 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM2 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. In Manhattan, the proposed BM2 would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7, BM8, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
### [p385_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p385_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
[p385_c0016] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p385_c0017] Spans and inbound frequencies would remain the same as the existing BM2 Downtown variant. Outbound frequencies would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p385_c0018] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p385_c0019] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p385_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 7:00 AM - 9:00 AM - 24 - - - Outbound 3:00 PM - 8:00 PM - - 60 45 60
```
[p385_c0021] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p385_c0022] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 390

### [p390_c0001] Express
### [p390_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p390_c0003] BM2c Canarsie - Downtown/Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM2, BM7c, BM8c, M55
### [p390_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p390_c0005] Existing: 19.2 miles Proposed: 19.2 miles
### [p390_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p390_c0007] Existing: 1,449 feet Proposed: 1,802 feet
### [p390_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p390_c0009] Existing: 1.0 per mile Proposed: 0.9 per mile
### [p390_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p390_c0011] The proposed BM2c would refer to the Downtown/Midtown off-peak “combo” variant of the existing BM2. The proposed BM2c would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM2c would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. In Manhattan, the proposed BM2c would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7c, BM8c, the M55, and numerous subway lines.
[p390_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p390_c0013] Weekday spans would be shortened and outbound frequencies reduced due to low ridership on some trips. Saturday service would be discontinued due to low ridership.
### [p390_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p390_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p390_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p390_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p390_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM - 5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM - 8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM - 3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM - 6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM - 11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 5:00 AM - 7:00 AM 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM 60 60 60 - - Outbound 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM - - - - 60
```
[p390_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p390_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 395

### [p395_c0001] Express
### [p395_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p395_c0003] BM3
### [p395_c0004] Sheepshead Bay - Downtown Manhattan
[p395_c0005] Related Routes: BM3, BM4, BM7, BM8, M55
### [p395_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p395_c0007] Existing: 15.1 miles Proposed: 14.1 miles
### [p395_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p395_c0009] Existing: 1,448 feet Proposed: 1,686 feet
### [p395_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p395_c0011] Existing: 1.1 per mile Proposed: 1.0 per mile
### [p395_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p395_c0013] The proposed BM3 would refer only to the Downtown peak-only variant of the existing BM3. The proposed BM3 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM3 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. Between Beverley Rd and the Prospect Expwy, the BM3 would follow the same more direct non-stop travel path as the BM1 and BM2 via Park Circle inbound and Canton Av outbound. Service on Coney Island Av and Church Av would still be provided by the BM4/BM4c/BM34. In Manhattan, the proposed BM3 would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7/BM7c, BM8/BM8c, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
[p395_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p395_c0015] Frequencies and the inbound span would remain the same as the existing BM3 Downtown variant. The outbound span would be shortened due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p395_c0016] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p395_c0017] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns
### [p395_c0018] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p395_c0019] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p395_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 7:30 AM - 9:00 AM - 23 - - - Outbound 4:30 PM - 6:30 PM - - - 30 -
```
[p395_c0021] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p395_c0022] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

### [p400_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p400_c0011] The proposed BM3c would refer to the Downtown/Midtown off-peak "combo" variant of the existing BM3. The proposed BM3c would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM3c would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. Between Beverley Rd and the Prospect Expwy, the BM3c would follow the same more direct non-stop travel path as the BM1 and BM2 via Park Circle inbound and Caton Av outbound. Service on Coney Island Av and Church Av would still be provided by the BM4/BM4c/BM34. In Manhattan, the proposed BM3c would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7/BM7c, BM8/BM8c, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
[p400_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p400_c0013] Weekday spans would be shortened and frequencies reduced due to low ridership on some trips. Saturday service would be discontinued due to low ridership.
### [p400_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p400_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns

## Page 405

### [p405_c0001] Express
### [p405_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p405_c0003] BM4
### [p405_c0004] Gerritsen Beach - Downtown Manhattan
[p405_c0005] Related Routes: BM4, BM1, BM3, BM7, BM8, M55
### [p405_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p405_c0007] Existing: 13.6 miles Proposed: 12.7 miles
### [p405_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p405_c0009] Existing: 1,367 feet Proposed: 1,802 feet
### [p405_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p405_c0011] Existing: 1.1 per mile Proposed: 0.8 per mile
### [p405_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p405_c0013] The proposed BM4 would refer only to the Downtown peak-only variant of the existing BM4. The proposed BM4 would be rerouted to travel via Avenue L and Coney Island Av instead of Avenue K, Ocean Av, and Cortelyou Rd. The new route path removes two turns, thereby reducing delays, and it provides new express service to Coney Island Av. Service on Ocean Av would still be provided by the BM1 and BM3. Existing service on Avenue K between Nostrand Av and Ocean Av would be discontinued. Existing express service on Cortelyou Rd would be discontinued. In Manhattan, the proposed BM4 would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7, BM8, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
[p405_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p405_c0015] Frequencies and inbound span would remain the same as the existing BM4 Downtown variant. The outbound span would be shortened due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p405_c0016] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p405_c0017] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p405_c0018] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p405_c0019] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p405_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM - 5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM - 8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM - 3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM - 6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM - 11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 7:30 AM - 9:00 AM - 23 - - - Outbound 4:30 PM - 6:30 PM - - - 30 -
```
[p405_c0021] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p405_c0022] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

> [p406_c0006] The map illustrates the proposed Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign, specifically the BM4 route. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating an 'Added Service'. It starts at Gerritsen Beach and proceeds through Marine Park, Mill Basin, and various residential areas in Brooklyn, including Flatlands and Midwood. The route then crosses the East River into Manhattan, following Avenue K and Avenue L. A dashed black line indicates a 'Discontinued Service' segment on Ocean Av. A green callout box notes that this segment would be served by the proposed BM1 and BM3 routes. The map includes a legend for service types (Existing, Added, Non-Stop, Discontinued), bus network, subway, accessible stations, future accessible stations, LIRR, and institutional land uses. A scale bar shows 0, 0.5, and 1 mile, and a north arrow is present. An inset map shows the location of the study area within the New York City metropolitan region.

### [p410_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p410_c0005] Existing: 18.5 miles Proposed: 18.3 miles
### [p410_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p410_c0007] Existing: 1,405 feet Proposed: 1,770 feet
### [p410_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p410_c0009] Existing: 0.9 per mile Proposed: 0.7 per mile
### [p410_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p410_c0011] The proposed BM4c would refer to the Downtown/Midtown off-peak “combo” variant of the existing BM4. The proposed BM4c would be rerouted to travel via Avenue L and Coney Island Av instead of Avenue K, Ocean Av, and Cortelyou Rd. The new route path removes two turns, thereby reducing delays, and it provides new express service to Coney Island Av. Service on Ocean Av would still be provided by the BM1c and BM3c. Existing service on Avenue K between Nostrand Av and Ocean Av would be discontinued. Existing express service on Cortelyou Rd would be discontinued. In Manhattan, the proposed BM4c would travel in both directions on Water St in order to simplify service. Service on Trinity Pl and Church St would be provided by the proposed BM7c, BM8c, the M55, and numerous nearby subway lines.
[p410_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p410_c0013] Weekday spans would be shortened and inbound frequencies reduced due to low ridership on some trips. Saturday service would be discontinued due to low ridership.
### [p410_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p410_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p410_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p410_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p410_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:00 AM - 7:00 AM 8:30 AM - 10:00 AM - 30 60 - - Outbound 6:30 PM - 8:00 PM - - - 30 60
```
[p410_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p410_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

### [p415_c0003] BM7
### [p415_c0004] Bay Ridge - Downtown Manhattan
[p415_c0005] Related Routes: X27, M55
### [p415_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p415_c0007] Existing: 12.1 miles Proposed: 9.9 miles
### [p415_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p415_c0009] Existing: 1,243 feet Proposed: 1,371 feet

[p415_c0011] Existing: 1.0 per mile Proposed: 0.9 per mile
### [p415_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p415_c0013] The Downtown peak-only variant of the existing X27 would be re-labeled as the BM7 to be consistent with most express routes. The existing route path would be maintained. However, all trips would terminate at Duane St/Thomas St due to low ridership between Duane St/Thomas St and 23 St. Service on 6 Av would be provided by the M55 and numerous nearby subway lines.
### [p415_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p415_c0015] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns
[p415_c0016] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p415_c0017] Spans would remain the same as the existing X27 Downtown variant, but frequencies would be slightly decreased inbound due to low ridership on some trips.
