## Page 424

### [p424_c0001] Express
### [p424_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p424_c0003] BM8
### [p424_c0004] Gravesend - Downtown Manhattan
[p424_c0005] Related Routes: X28, B36, B74, M55
### [p424_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p424_c0007] Existing: 15.7 miles Proposed: 11.8 miles
### [p424_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p424_c0009] Existing: 1,435 feet Proposed: 1,602 feet
### [p424_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p424_c0011] Existing: 0.9 per mile Proposed: 0.5 per mile
### [p424_c0012] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p424_c0013] The Downtown peak-only variant of the existing X28 would be re-labeled as the BM8 to be consistent with most express routes. The existing route path would be maintained. However, all trips would terminate at Cropsey Av/Canal Av due to low ridership in Sea Gate. Service in the area would be provided by connecting from the B36 or B74 to the D/F/N/Q subway lines. Also, all trips would terminate at Duane St/Thomas St due to low ridership between Duane St/Thomas St and 23 St. Service on 6 Av would be provided by the M55 and numerous nearby subway lines.
### [p424_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p424_c0015] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p424_c0016] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p424_c0017] Spans would remain the same as the existing X28 Downtown variant, but frequencies would be slightly decreased due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p424_c0018] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p424_c0019] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p424_c0020]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:30 AM - 9:00 AM - 10 - - - Outbound 4:00 PM - 7:30 PM - - - 13 15
```
[p424_c0021] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p424_c0022] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

The route is shown as a solid green line, indicating 'Existing Service'. A legend in the upper right corner defines the map's symbology: solid green for existing service, double green for added service, double grey for non-stop sections, dashed black for discontinued service, solid grey for the proposed bus network, blue circles for subway stations, grey circles for accessible stations, grey circles with a dot for future accessible stations, grey squares for LIRR stations, and tan shading for institutional land uses. The map also shows various bus stops, including accessible ones, and major streets such as the Goyanous Expressway, 14th Avenue, 88th Street, and 15th Street. A circular inset map in the bottom left corner shows the route's location within the New York City metropolitan area. ## Page 429

### [p429_c0001] Express
### [p429_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p429_c0003] BM8c Gravesend - Downtown/Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: X28, B36, B74
### [p429_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p429_c0005] Existing: 17.5 miles Proposed: 15.5 miles
### [p429_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p429_c0007] Existing: 1,409 feet Proposed: 1,642 feet
### [p429_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p429_c0009] Existing: 0.8 per mile Proposed: 0.6 per mile
### [p429_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p429_c0011] The Downtown/Midtown off-peak “combo” variant of the existing X28 would be re-labeled as the BM8c to be consistent with most express routes. The existing route path would be maintained. However, all trips would terminate at Cropsey Av/Canal Av due to low ridership in Sea Gate. Service in the area would be provided by connecting from the B36 or B74 to the D/F/N/Q subway lines.
### [p429_c0012] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p429_c0013] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p429_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p429_c0015] Spans would be shortened and frequencies on weekends would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p429_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p429_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

## Page 434

### [p434_c0001] Express
### [p434_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p434_c0003] BM31 Mill Basin - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM1
### [p434_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p434_c0005] Existing: 19.7 miles Proposed: 19.3 miles
### [p434_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p434_c0007] Existing: 1,308 feet Proposed: 1,631 feet
### [p434_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p434_c0009] Existing: 0.9 per mile Proposed: 0.9 per mile
### [p434_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p434_c0011] The proposed BM31 would refer to the Midtown peak-only variant of the existing BM1. The proposed BM31 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Flatbush Av and Ocean Av, the BM31 would use Avenue J instead of Avenue K in order to avoid a difficult series of turns at Avenue K/Flatbush Av. Existing service would be discontinued on this portion of Avenue K. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM31 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd.
[p434_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p434_c0013] Spans would remain the same as the existing BM1 Midtown variant. Frequencies would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p434_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p434_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p434_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p434_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p434_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:00 AM - 9:00 AM - 23 - - - Outbound 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM - - 60 30 -
```
[p434_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p434_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

> [p435_c0005] The map illustrates the BM31 bus route in Brooklyn, connecting Midtown Manhattan to Mill Basin. The route is shown in green, indicating it is a new service. It starts at Prospect Park and proceeds south through McDonald Av, Corley Island Av, and Corleyou Rd. It then turns east on Ocean Av, passing through Avenue J and Avenue K. The route continues east on Ralph Av and Veterans Av, then turns south on E 66 St and 56 Dr, ending at the waterfront near Mill Basin. The map also shows the existing bus network in grey, subway stations in blue circles, and accessible stations in dark grey circles. Institutional land uses are highlighted in orange. A legend in the top right corner provides details on route types, service changes, and map symbols. A scale bar at the bottom right indicates distances up to 1 mile, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the study area within the greater New York City region.

## Page 439

### [p439_c0001] Express
### [p439_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p439_c0003] BM32 Canarsie - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM2
### [p439_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p439_c0005] Existing: 19.3 miles Proposed: 19.1 miles
### [p439_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p439_c0007] Existing: 1,450 feet Proposed: 1,883 feet
### [p439_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p439_c0009] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.7 per mile
### [p439_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p439_c0011] The proposed BM32 would refer to the Midtown peak-only variant of the existing BM2. The proposed BM32 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM32 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd.
### [p439_c0012] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p439_c0013] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
[p439_c0014] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p439_c0015] The inbound span would remain the same as the existing BM2 Midtown variant. The outbound span would be shortened and frequencies in both directions would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.

[p439_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

## Page 444

### [p444_c0001] Express
### [p444_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p444_c0003] BM33 Sheepshead Bay - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM3, BM34
### [p444_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p444_c0005] Existing: 20.0 miles Proposed: 19.7 miles
### [p444_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p444_c0007] Existing: 1,476 feet Proposed: 1,765 feet
### [p444_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p444_c0009] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.7 per mile
### [p444_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p444_c0011] The proposed BM33 would refer to the Midtown peak-only variant of the existing BM3. The proposed BM33 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Ocean Av and Coney Island Av, the BM33 would use Beverley Rd instead of Cortelyou Rd, a shorter route path that would remove a challenging turn at Coney Island Av/Cortelyou Rd, reducing delays. Existing express service would be discontinued on Cortelyou Rd. Between Beverley Rd and the Prospect Expwy, the BM33 would follow the same more direct non-stop travel path as the BM1 and BM2 via Park Circle inbound and Caton Av outbound. Service on Coney Island Av and Church Av would still be provided by the BM4/BM4c/BM34.
[p444_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p444_c0013] Spans and inbound frequencies would remain the same as the existing BM3 Midtown variant. Outbound frequencies would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p444_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p444_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns
### [p444_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p444_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p444_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 7:00 AM - 9:00 AM - 20 - - - Outbound 4:00 PM - 7:00 PM - - - 36 -
```
[p444_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p444_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 449

### [p449_c0001] Express
### [p449_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p449_c0003] BM34 Gerritsen Beach - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM4, BM31, BM33
### [p449_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p449_c0005] Existing: 18.6 miles Proposed: 18.3 miles
### [p449_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p449_c0007] Existing: 1,555 feet Proposed: 1,819 feet
### [p449_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p449_c0009] Existing: 0.6 per mile Proposed: 0.5 per mile
### [p449_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p449_c0011] The proposed BM34 would refer to the Midtown peak-only variant of the existing BM4. The proposed BM34 would be rerouted to travel via Avenue L and Coney Island Av instead of Avenue K, Ocean Av, and Cortelyou Rd. The new route path removes two turns, thereby reducing delays, and it provides new express service to Coney Island Av. Service on Ocean Av would still be provided by the BM31 and BM33. Existing service on Avenue K between Nostrand Av and Ocean Av would be discontinued. Existing express service on Cortelyou Rd would be discontinued.
[p449_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p449_c0013] The inbound span would remain the same as the existing BM4 Midtown variant. The outbound span would be shortened and frequencies in both directions would be reduced due to low ridership on some trips.
### [p449_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p449_c0015] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p449_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p449_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p449_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 7:00 AM - 8:30 AM - 30 - - - Outbound 4:30 PM - 6:00 PM - - - 30 -
```
[p449_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p449_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 454

### [p454_c0001] Express
### [p454_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p454_c0003] BM35 Spring Creek - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: BM5
### [p454_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p454_c0005] Existing: 16.9 miles Proposed: 16.9 miles
### [p454_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p454_c0007] Existing: 1,662 feet Proposed: 2,115 feet
### [p454_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p454_c0009] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.7 per mile
### [p454_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p454_c0011] The BM5 would be re-labeled as the BM35 to be consistent with other Brooklyn Express routes to Midtown Manhattan. The existing route path would be maintained.
[p454_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p454_c0013] Weekday frequencies would remain the same as the existing BM5. Weekday spans would be shortened due to low ridership on some trips. Saturday service would be discontinued due to low ridership.
### [p454_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p454_c0015] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p454_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p454_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p454_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:00 AM - 10:00 AM - 23 60 - - Outbound 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM - - 60 26 45
```
[p454_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p454_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 462

### [p462_c0001] Express
### [p462_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p462_c0003] BM38 Gravesend - Midtown Manhattan Related Routes: X38, B36, B74
### [p462_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p462_c0005] Existing: 19.2 miles Proposed: 17.5 miles
### [p462_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING 1
[p462_c0007] Existing: 1,386 feet Proposed: 1,575 feet
### [p462_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p462_c0009] Existing: 0.6 per mile Proposed: 0.4 per mile
### [p462_c0010] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p462_c0011] The X38 would be re-labeled as the BM38 to be consistent with most express routes. The existing route path would be maintained. However, all trips would terminate at Cropsey Av/Canal Av due to low ridership in Sea Gate. Service in the area would be provided by connecting from the B36 or B74 to the D/F/N/Q subway lines.
[p462_c0012] As an Express route, stops on local streets would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up travel time and improve reliability. Along the highway, Express routes travel non-stop to and from Manhattan.
[p462_c0013] Frequencies and spans would remain the same as the existing X38, other than a slight increase in inbound frequency and a slight decrease in outbound frequency.
### [p462_c0014] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p462_c0015] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p462_c0016] PROPOSED FREQUENCY AND SPAN 2
[p462_c0017] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p462_c0018]

```text
Service Hours Early AM 5:00 AM-5:59 AM AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-3:59 PM PM Peak 4:00 PM-6:59 PM Evening 7:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY Inbound 6:30 AM - 9:00 AM - 9 - - - Outbound 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM - - 60 15 -
```
[p462_c0019] _1 Excludes non-stop sections._
[p462_c0020] _2 Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes; spans refer to trip times at the last stop before the non-stop section._

## Page 467

### [p467_c0001] Glossary Of Terms
[p467_c0002] Accessible – A service, vehicle, or facility is accessible if it is in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) , or in general (nonlegal) terms if it is readily usable by persons with disabilities.
[p467_c0003] Alighting – Exiting or getting off of a bus, train, or other mode of transit. See Boarding.
[p467_c0004] ACS – American Community Survey. An ongoing, nationwide survey conducted by the U.S. Census Bureau from which data on employment, demographics, commuting behavior, and other subjects is gathered and distributed.
[p467_c0005] ADA – The American with Disabilities Act of 1990, which applied to public transit requires that transit providers must follow regulations ensuring that services, vehicles, and facilities are accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities. See Accessible.
[p467_c0006] BRT – Bus Rapid Transit. BRT systems strive to bring faster, more reliable, and quality bus service to high ridership corridors by combining amenities of rail-based rapid transit systems with the flexibility of buses. New York City Transit's implementation of BRT is Select Bus Service, which improves speed and reliability through dedicated bus lanes , off-board fare payment, stop spacing , and transit signal priority .
[p467_c0007] Boarding – Entering or getting onto a bus, train, or other mode of transit. See Alighting.
[p467_c0008] Bus bulb – A sidewalk platform extending from the sidewalk that enables easier boarding for bus passengers. Bus bulbs are as close to level with the floor of the bus as feasible.
[p467_c0009] Bus lane – A lane of the roadway dedicated exclusively to bus movement.
[p467_c0010] Bus network – A collection of bus routes, including the physical paths they take as well as their scheduled frequencies and spans of service. In essence, where buses travel, when buses travel, and how often buses travel.
[p467_c0011] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 466_

## Page 468

### [p468_c0001] Glossary Of Terms
[p468_c0002] Bus priority – Any number of techniques or tools that enable bus transit to take precedence over other modes of surface transportation in traffic.
[p468_c0003] With transit signal priority (TSP) , traffic lights can change more quickly from red to green or a green light can be held longer if a bus is approaching.
[p468_c0004] CBDT – Central Business District Tolling program.
[p468_c0005] CJTP – Customer Journey Time Performance. The percentage of customers whose journeys (trips) are completed within five minutes of the scheduled time. CJTP considers both how long customers wait at the bus stop beyond what they would have if their bus arrived on time, as well as how long customers spend on the bus beyond what they would have if the bus completed its trip in the time allotted in the schedule.
[p468_c0006] Connections – When a rider transfers from one MTA vehicle to another.
[p468_c0007] Core route – A route in a bus network that tends to have higher ridership and higher frequency and that provides critical connections to key destinations.
[p468_c0008] Crosstown (SBS) routes – See detailed description on page 29.
[p468_c0009] Express bus service – Bus service focused specifically on transporting commuters between Manhattan and the outer boroughs. Express bus routes typically have a series of pick-up locations in one borough and a series of drop-off locations in the other, between which is an express segment. The bus does not stop throughout the express segment, which is generally on a highway. Express bus service charges a premium fare.
[p468_c0010] Frequency – how often a bus runs on a route. The frequencies in this Draft Plan indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction (the direction carrying the most customers), in minutes.
[p468_c0011] Frequent All-Day Service - service that comes every ten minutes or better from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM on weekdays.
[p468_c0012] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 467_

## Page 469

### [p469_c0001] Glossary Of Terms
[p469_c0002] Inaccessible – A service, vehicle, or facility is inaccessible if it is not in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), or in general (nonlegal) terms if it is not readily usable by persons with disabilities. See Accessible.
[p469_c0003] Interborough service – Transit service that can take a customer from one borough into another without transferring to another route or mode.
[p469_c0004] Limited routes – See detailed description on page 28.
[p469_c0005] Local routes – See detailed description on page 26. Also refers to all routes that are not Express routes.
[p469_c0006] MetroCard – The Metropolitan Transportation Authority’s fare payment method.
[p469_c0007] NYC DOT – New York City Department of Transportation.
[p469_c0008] NYPD – New York Police Department.
[p469_c0009] OMNY – The MTA’s new contactless fare payment system. Customers can use contactless debit and credit cards, as well as smart devices, to pay their fare. All buses and subway stations are equipped with OMNY readers. Visit https://new.mta.info/fares/omny for more information.
[p469_c0010] On-Time Performance – Measures how well a bus route performs compared to its schedule. It is defined as the percentage of buses that are between one minute early and five minutes late as compared to the schedule at each official timepoint along the route.
[p469_c0011] Peak – The times during which commuter demand is heaviest and typically when the most service is provided. The morning peak period is weekdays between 6:00 AM and 9:00 AM. The afternoon peak period on Local routes is weekdays between 2:00 PM and 6:00 PM; the afternoon peak period on Express routes is weekdays between 4:00 PM and 7:00 PM.
[p469_c0012] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 468_

## Page 470

### [p470_c0001] Glossary Of Terms
[p470_c0002] Priority Corridor – Key corridors identified by NYC DOT where bus priority street treatments can be implemented to better support sustainable, all-day bus service.
[p470_c0003] Productivity – The measure of ridership given the level of service provided. Bus routes are more productive when they attract more riders per unit of time that they are in service.
[p470_c0004] Ridership – The total number of customers using a specific route or the bus system generally.
[p470_c0005] Real-Time Passenger Information Signs – provides riders with wait time information for buses and projects the data onto an easy-to-see LED display for customers.
[p470_c0006] Remix by Via – An interactive web-based transportation planning software used to help plan, share, and receive feedback on our Draft Plan bus network.
[p470_c0007] Running time – The length of time it takes a bus to travel from one terminal of a route to the other.
[p470_c0008] Rush routes – See detailed description on page 27.
[p470_c0009] SBS routes – Select Bus Service. New York City Transit's branded implementation of BRT (Bus Rapid Transit) See detailed description on page 29.
[p470_c0010] Span – The time period during the day that a route is in service.
[p470_c0011] Stop spacing – The average traveled distance between consecutive bus stops along a route.
[p470_c0012] TSP – Traffic signal priority. See bus priority.
[p470_c0013] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 469_
